headline: Renewed US-Iran hostilities highlight Tehran's deep mistrust of negotiations
description: The exchanges of strikes in recent hours have bolstered the arguments of the ultraconservative wing of Iran's theocratic regime, which regards talks with Washington as criminal. The cease-fire is hanging by a thread.

headline: How Ukraine is turning Crimea, the Russians' Riviera, into a battlefield
Le Pen cuts short first presidential campaign trip amid protests
Graham Platner, Democratic candidate for US Senate, exits race after rape allegation
description: Platner, the Democratic Senate nominee for Maine and the party's hope to flip a Republican-held seat, ended his campaign following a rape allegation. He has denied the accusations.

headline: EWC 2026 in Paris: Behind the scenes of the controversial partnership between France and Saudi Arabia in esports
description: By hosting this Saudi video game tournament in Paris, with an opening ceremony on Wednesday, July 8, featuring a concert by Aya Nakamura, the Elysée is endorsing the kingdom's dominance in the sector – but not without facing criticism from the French esports community.

headline: Kylian Mbappé: A leader on a mission, both on the pitch and behind the scenes at the 2026 World Cup
description: The captain of the French team, which faces Morocco on Thursday in the quarterfinals, keeps scoring goals while playing an active role behind the scenes. His selflessness stands in stark contrast to the criticisms of his supposed individualism.

headline: 'A World Cup is not a reflection of international relations, or if it is, it is a particularly distorted one'
description: Aside from the ban imposed on Russia by European and international football authorities after its invasion of Ukraine in 2022, the geopolitics of football has little to do with geopolitics in general, even though the tournament offers plenty of lessons, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

Marine Le Pen, phoenix of French far right, embarks on Elysée race
description: Marine Le Pen announced she would run for president hours after her embezzlement conviction was upheld.

headline: Somaliland's high-risk quest for recognition
description: The self-proclaimed republic aspires to accede to the international stage. It finds itself at the center of a game of alliances, caught between Israeli, American and Emirati ambitions and threats from Yemen and neighboring Somalia.

headline: The day Marine Le Pen took back control of her fate, despite a conviction
description: After being sentenced to one year of house arrest, the French far right's leader found an opening back into the presidential race.

headline: Marine Le Pen's shifting principles
